commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=8979819e2f343b1b4efc8adf1807f0f415181954)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds an optional Claude CLI JSONL parser callback for commentary before tool-use blocks, wires it through regular and live Claude CLI execution, and adds parser tests.

PR surface: Source +50, Tests +137. Total +187 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: a Claude JSONL sequence of text_delta, tool_use, another text_delta, and another tool_use will emit the cumulative assistant text at the second boundary. I did not run tests because this review is read-only, but the parser and compositor path is direct.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed for the cumulative-emission repair and real behavior proof; automation should not take over an external PR that still lacks after-fix proof.

target.title: feat(cli): emit commentary progress events from Claude CLI parser
target.body:
## Summary

- Segment assistant text at tool_use boundaries in `createCliJsonlStreamingParser`
- Emit `onItemEvent({ kind: "preamble" })` for inter-tool commentary, matching the Codex backend's existing behavior
- Plugs directly into the commentary progress system merged in #87072 -- zero channel-side changes needed

## Motivation

The P2 commentary draft system (#87072) only receives events from Codex today. Claude CLI accumulates all assistant text into one blob with no phase segmentation. This PR makes Claude CLI emit the same preamble events, so Claude users get tool commentary progress too.

## Files changed

- `src/agents/cli-output.ts` -- `onCommentaryText` callback + dedup tracking
- `src/agents/cli-runner/execute.ts` -- wire callback to emit preamble agent events
- `src/agents/cli-runner/claude-live-session.ts` -- pass callback through live session path
- `src/agents/cli-output.test.ts` -- 3 test cases

## Test plan

- [x] Existing 25 cli-output tests pass
- [x] Commentary fires when text precedes tool_use
- [x] No emission when no text precedes tool_use
- [x] Deduplicates consecutive tool_use without intervening text
